tldr: Adrien — you have two live ads in your Meta Library right now, and they are functionally the same ad: identical headline, identical body, identical CTA, identical landing page, both carousels, both shipped within 24 hours of each other and both running past day 60. Under Meta's Andromeda retrieval engine, one concept across two carousels is below the platform's diversity floor, and the algorithm has almost certainly clustered them into a single served unit.

benchmark.context: At your scale, the typical apparel brand refreshes hero creative every ~21 days and top performers refresh every ~14. You're sitting at one distinct concept across two ads — below Meta's Andromeda 5-concept floor — and both have been running for over 60 days, which is roughly double the window your category sustains before CPM drift kicks in.

ads.headline: RIVIR Comfy Travel Sets
ads.body: 20% Off Now, Style & Comfort On the Go.
drivers: Day 64 on the same carousel — more than double your category's 28-day refresh window, and Andromeda's retrieval clustering treats this ad and its twin as one served creative.
Headline 'RIVIR Comfy Travel Sets' is descriptive, not a hook — no specificity, no transformation, no reason to stop scrolling; reads like a category label.
Body copy '20% Off Now, Style & Comfort On the Go.' is duplicated verbatim with your other live ad — the algorithm sees zero variant signal across the account.

ads.headline: RIVIR Comfy Travel Sets
ads.body: 20% Off Now, Style & Comfort On the Go.
drivers: Day 63, launched within 24 hours of your other carousel — this is a textbook burst-and-stall: two ads shipped at once and nothing fresh since.

100% copy duplication with the sibling carousel — same headline, same body, same CTA, same landing collection — so Meta's clustering serves whichever has marginally better engagement and starves the other.
Carousel-only format mix across the entire account means you have no static or video lane to A/B against; if carousel fatigues, you have nothing in queue to absorb spend.

hero_concept.hook: One dress. Seven outfits. Zero wrinkles.
hero_concept.headline: Pack lighter. Look better.
hero_concept.primary_text:
RIVIR was built for the woman who lives between gates and dinner reservations — pieces that don't crease in a carry-on and don't look like travel clothes when you arrive.

The Fiora Bloom Printed Strap Dress is the one we'd put in your bag first: lightweight, packable, the kind of print that reads dressed-up at dinner and effortless at brunch. Style it three ways without unpacking the rest of your suitcase.

20% off your first order — shop the Travel Essentials edit.
hero_concept.visual_direction: Editorial flat-lay of the Fiora Bloom dress folded neatly inside an open carry-on suitcase alongside sandals, sunglasses and a linen scarf, shot from directly overhead on a warm sand-toned linen surface.
hero_concept.fills_gap: Both of your live carousels lean on the same generic 'Comfy Travel Sets / 20% Off' message with no specific product, no hook, and no visual point of view. This concept gives Meta a different format (static, not carousel), a different angle (one-product storytelling vs. category promo), and a hook that names a benefit ('seven outfits, zero wrinkles') instead of describing the category.
hero_concept.image_prompt: A photorealistic editorial product photograph featuring the product from the reference image, styled in an open hard-shell carry-on suitcase scene with the dress folded neatly on top of a packed-but-tidy interior, accessorized with woven sandals, tortoise sunglasses, and a folded linen scarf, with the surrounding surface a warm sand-toned linen tablecloth, with soft diffused natural daylight from a window at camera-left casting gentle shadows. Warm, sun-washed editorial travel aesthetic — think slow-morning Mediterranean hotel room, premium DTC fashion catalog tone. Square 1:1 composition with breathing room top and bottom for ad text overlay. CRITICAL — preserve the exact floral 'Fiora Bloom' printed pattern, colorway, and lightweight flowy fabric of the strap dress exactly as shown in the reference image — strictly NO color shift, NO smoothing the floral print to a solid, NO replacing the floral motif with a different pattern, NO darkening or oversaturating the print, NO warm-tone bias that would alter the bloom colors. STRUCTURE — preserve the exact construction: thin spaghetti shoulder straps (two straps, one per shoulder), straight or lightly curved neckline as shown, fitted bodice flowing into a relaxed A-line/midi skirt, all-over floral 'Fiora Bloom' print exactly as positioned on the reference garment, no visible buttons or zippers on the front, no pockets added. Do NOT thicken the straps, do NOT convert to a halter or one-shoulder, do NOT add sleeves, do NOT add pockets, ruffles, tiers, or trim not in the reference, do NOT alter the silhouette or hem length, do NOT remove or modify the floral print. Render the product's fabric, drape, print, and every visible construction detail (silhouette, strap count, neckline, hemline, printed floral pattern) EXACTLY as they appear in the reference image. Do NOT change the color temperature, do NOT shift the print, do NOT introduce tones not present in the reference, do NOT add or remove straps, buttons, or hardware, do NOT alter the silhouette. IMPORTANT: the 'no text' and 'no logos' items in the NEGATIVE list below apply ONLY to text or logos added as overlay to the photograph itself — text, logos, graphics, prints, and brand labels that exist ON the product in the reference image are part of the product and must be preserved exactly. Premium commerce photography quality. NEGATIVE: no overlay text added to the photograph, no overlay logos added to the photograph, no watermarks, no UI elements, no Facebook interface, no clickable buttons, no faces, no models, no collage, no borders, no duplicate products, no color shift, no metal swap, no silver-to-gold or gold-to-silver conversion, no warm-tone bias on cool metals, no recoloring of fabric or stones, no pattern modifications, no removing or replacing the product's printed graphics, no extra hardware not present in reference, no added pockets or pleats, no silhouette alterations, no design modifications.
hero_concept.reference_title: The Fiora Bloom Printed Strap Dress

next_step.urgency: Both live ads cross practical fatigue in the next 5 days.
next_step.headline: Walk through the RIVIR refresh plan with Kyle
next_step.body:
Adrien, in this 30 min call, we'll map out:
• 3 customer avatars at your price point
• The motivational hook driving each
• A 30-day creative + spend roadmap (to outbeat your current ROAS)

Yours to keep — work with us or don't.

method_note: We went through every live ad in your Meta Ad Library, mapped format and copy patterns against Meta's 2025–2026 Andromeda diversity rules, and benchmarked refresh cadence against apparel norms — no account access used.
